Basically anytime bedding or towels are washed, the washer will be off balanced and create a racket with the wash tub hitting the inside of the washer wall.

I always get concerned when I get devices that require WiFi and an "app" in order to get them to work. This one though was simple and straightforward. I had not heard of the brand before but got a much better level of comfort when I went onto the Google Play Store to download the app and saw that it has been downloaded over 10 Million Times!!!! I literally downloaded the app, pushed the button on the device and it instantly connected up and let me choose what WiFi network I wanted it to connect up to. Once that was chosen, it connected and I was done. I put this sensor in a remote location, so when I got home, I opened up the app and saw that it was indeed updating the temperature and humidity to the app remotely. You can setup alerts based on your needs and you can change how often the sensor updates up to the cloud. Overall, a really cool (and tiny) device for monitoring the temp and humidity while you are away.

I am an LG TV and Phone fan, love them, and thought the same quality would carry over to their appliances.
I filled a new kitchen with LG appliances and will review them individually. I use this range several times a day.
-As an avid baker, I realized right off the bat the temperature was incorrect in the oven, which required repairs and replacement of the thermostat (while still brand new).
-The burners have issues - the front right burner is the most powerful but hard to get to a "medium" temp, as there are 2 sets of fire rings, one will often go out if not set high enough and will just release gas.
-All the burners tend to lose flame on low and simply release gas, so you must be vigilant about checking flames on that setting.
-The middle burner is absolutely useless. You can perhaps use it as a cigarette lighter.
-It comes with a griddle to fit over it (which is Teflon by the way, so don't use it) but it doesn't get hot enough to cook anything. The shape of the burner limits it to the Teflon griddle, so it's pretty much a useless burner. Google this, it's a common complaint with LG's middle burner. Consider this a 4 burner range instead of 5 burner.
-The knobs are not metal, they are plastic.
-Two of the burners have a weird flame output, so you have to put them on low for a while before increasing to medium or high.
-ALSO, please know that the pretty blue paint in the oven will crack. Expect it. The serviceperson advised he can try to replace this *known* problem, but LG considers this cosmetic and will not replace it. Also the paint on the replacement would likely also shatter as well. Google this as well before purchasing, it's a common complaint.
-It took months of regular, daily use for burnt oil smell to go away. I was told it would burn off after a couple uses, this was not the case. I don't know why they applied this oil to the oven, but it was very unpleasant.
-The convection fan will only kick on once the set temperature is reached, which is an unnecessary delay. Once the oven is on, you shouldn't need to wait the 12 or 15 minutes for the walls of the oven to achieve preheated temperature with convection as its purpose is to circulate the immediately existing heated air. I have used better convection ranges with a lower price tag.
My impression on this range is it is priced above it's quality. There are seeming bells and whistles, but they are cheap and don't last.

Fixed the DE error code on my 5 year old LG front loader washing machine for a fraction of the repairman estimate. HOWEVER, you need the right tool. Washer Spring Expansion Removal Tool is a must. simply remove some screws from rear of top cover and slide cover back to see what you are doing. use the tool to remove spring holding rubber boot around door seal. remove the 2 screws by door lock. slide off section of rubber boot by door lock. pull out door lock, remove electrical connector and attach to new door switch. reassemble in reverse. use some soapy water to ensure seal is in properly before reinstalling expansion spring. A little patience is needed, but it is a 30 minutes job, that saves you over $250.

I'm the type that tries several different versions of an item before settling on (what I hope to be) the best.

I purchased almost all the different "catch alls" sold directly by Amazon. One had a clip that attaches to your vent, but easily comes off if too much is added, and the rest had a wire you bent to attach them. I like the wired versions best, and this one rose above the others in usability.

It's a good size, but nowhere near "extra large" -- I had two others (made by different companies) that were bigger. However, this one is laid out the best of the bunch. One other model had an "outer" bag and an "inner" bag, sewn together at the neck -- the problem of course, is when you remove certain items, the "inner" bag kept coming out with the item you were retrieving -- thankfully, this particular catch all did NOT have that problem. The little mesh bag on the front of this catch all seemed more utilitarian than other bags as well. Others had the front bag stitched so tightly, it was difficult to remove the item (chap-stick or lighter) with just one hand while driving -- this catch all did not exhibit that problem, and was a breeze to use.

The only real gripe I have about this particular catch all is its size. It will easily hold a pack of smokes or a pair of sunglasses. It is not as deep as others (a pack if smokes will rise about 1/4" above the neck), but I like that -- digging around for small items in the bottom of a deeper bag while driving could be a problem. I wish it were wider (it has about 1/2" on either side left over with a pack of smokes in it) so that I could put several light-weight items in it -- instead, I got two and use one for sunglasses and one for smokes.

We original purchased this product for my elderly mother to help with her day to day. The word "easy" stands out.

Place it where ever you please as long as it properly has a place to drain to. Load it up, add detergent, close the hatch, turn on the unit, add water (or hook it up to your main line which is easier), then choose your dishwasher clean setting.

Pros:
Easy to set up and use. My mother is elderly and is currently battling cancer. While she cooks for her husband and herself, she's able to load all her dishes and cooking utensils into the unit and just have it clean overnight. She gets tired from little day to day activities and while we're able to do as much as we can for her, she still wants and needs to do things independently.

The controls are easy understand. You can choose from various settings and it's easy to understand which button does what. The unit will beep and boop depending on what's happening (like when it's filled with enough water for a wash cycle or when a wash cycle is finished), which is a nice feature.

It uses jets that spray out from the bottom like the fountains you'd find at Las Vegas and cleans very well. We haven't seen any food particles or any remaining debris on the plates or glasses after months of use.

All dishwashers will need to empty themselves. While it's able to filter and drain out run off, larger particles and food debris will remain in the filter which was easy to remove, clean, and replace. I have hand dexterity issues while my mother is elderly so we were surprised at how easy this was to do.

There was no issues with drying. Although you have to turn on the fan option with each cycle, the dishes and utensils came out dry and honestly, better than other dishwashers (our household has gone through 2 large scale family dishwashers and this smaller unit seems better at drying than those larger ones).

Very little detergent use. While most stores carry those detergent pods, you have to use loose detergent with this dishwasher. Overall, you'll be using less to clean.

Cons:
Small capacity. You won't be cleaning larger pots and pans in this thing. It's mostly made for smaller family units, college students, or the like. While my mom and her husband are able to use this freely and it meets their needs, others may find they need to use two or more wash cycles per day.

It's noisy. I would recommend using the dishwasher at night or before going to bed. While the fan is on to dry, you might hear a faint hum but it hasn't bothered us.

It uses a lot of water. With each cycle, you'll need to fill the unit up with the provided pitcher (or something similar). The unit will beep the moment it's full, but this can take up to four full pitchers to achieve. For myself, or anyone else, this can be time consuming and unwieldy as the take-in is at the top of the unit. We actually installed the fixed hose and I would recommend this option overall since it makes running the unit even more easier.

The dishwasher itself is bulky but that's not necessarily a bad thing. It needs to be close to the sink, or any other place it could drain to, but we had plenty of options in terms of placement with a generous hose length.

Overall our family is happy with this product!

Purchased two different cold brew bags at the same time and Madesco's was the second one tried.  Sanitized the bag as directed and let dry. Then filled with 4oz of coffee and synched the bag closed, after placing in a pitcher and filling with 48oz of cold filtered water, let set in refrigerator for 18 hours.  Last gently remove the bag of grounds from the pitcher and gave a gentle squeeze.  This bag seems to hold more water than the other brand and the only down side to the Madesco brand.  I recommend turning the bag wrong side out before filling with coffee, the used coffee grounds are easier to remove this way, rather than removing grounds from the raw hem. The coffee is so much better from the cold brew bags than any other way I've tried cold brewing.  Not sure if it is less handling of the grounds, but there has been no residue in my pitcher and I used expresso ground coffee.  I love the simplicity of this process.  I put my coffee pot in the recycle bin!  No more acidic coffee for me.  This may be my Christmas gift giving this year.  A cold brew bag and a pound of my favorite coffee, what a way to spread the word.

The k-cups work fine. There was a little bit of water seepage around the pod chamber but I think that was my user error. I packed the cup too tight. What I found works best is filling the cup very loosely to the edge with coarse grind, leveling off the top, and closing the lid. The water will be able to infuse the grounds more easily if it isn't packed tight. I use the strong setting. I have not found any loose grounds in my coffee mug so the seal is very secure. After I make my coffee, I pull the k-cup out and set it aside to cool. A few hours later I remember to come back and dump the used grounds. They slid right out into the trash. A quick warm water rinse in the sink and it's ready to go again. Set came with two k-cups so I can alternate their use. I'm very satisfied.

Basket filter:
I did a dry fit of the basket and it fits well. It's made of the same mesh material as the k-cups. I have not made coffee with it but I suspect it will work just fine with coarse grind.

How do I like my coffee? I prefer light roast coffees over dark roast. I like very strong coffee brewed rich. When I get to the bottom of the cup, I expect only a very small amount of coffee mud residue. The K-cups give me that perfect cup with an 8oz strong brew setting.

My husband and I want to express how happy we are with our new ice maker! We've only had it for about a month but we wonder why we didn't purchase this item sooner. We live in a tiny city apartment and so the size of the icemaker fits perfectly on our small kitchenette counter. It's sleek and the red compliments our other red appliances. It's simple to use and I love that you can select from small, medium or large sized ice. We turn it on only when we use it and after only 15-20 mins the icemaker generates approx 1.5-2 cups of medium sized ice. This is so much easier and faster than filling up ice trays and waiting for them to freeze. Overall, it's fairly quiet other than when the ice drops into the tray. We can't imagine hot summers without our new icemaker. I would highly recommend this item if you have a small kitchen space.

FYI, My husband and I purchased a French Door refrigerator model # : RF260BEAESR/AA on August 16, 2014. On the night of 9/28/2014, this refrigerator stopped providing cooling in both the freezer and the fridge compartments. This happened overnight. 9/28/2014 was a Sunday and I do my weekly food shopping on Sundays. So most of the groceries were lost. The cost of which was approximately $167.00. A major inconvenience, but this refrigerator is under warranty and it should be repaired quickly. The repairman came on 10/1/2014 and determined that both the main board of the refrigerator and the condenser fan were not functioning at all. Of course he did not have both parts, so the condenser fan had to be ordered. He returned on 10/7/2014 to replace the condenser fan. At that time we were without a functioning refrigerator for 10 days. So being reasonably intelligent people we purchased ice to keep the new groceries which we purchased, cold enough to consume. 10 days and we had to spend $60.00 on ice to keep our food cold enough for the family to eat. We contacted Samsung to submit a Food Loss inventory and bill. We were contacted and told that Samsung would not pay for the entire amount. We were told, quote... "Samsung will not pay for ice because it is water and Samsung does not pay for water." In addition Samsung will not pay for any food loss unless we agree to accept only the amount of $167.00. We contacted the Massachusetts Attorney General's office of Consumer Advocacy. When this office contacted Samsung to attempt to mediate the claim, Samsung rejected any contact. Now we purchased this refrigerator, a MAJOR APPLIANCE in every home and it FAILED in 6 weeks of usage. The condenser fan was put in improperly and the Main board computer system failed. In 6 Weeks! It seems that Samsung met it's requirement under the warranty to replace and repair, but is not at all concerned with the loss of usage of this major and necessary appliance for 10 days. This brand, new refrigerator.
Samsung is a multi-billion dollar company that will haggle with the customer even when the company reps knows they are wrong!

If you cook a lot, it doesn’t matter how big your kitchen is – it is NEVER big enough. I love to cook and often find myself running out of countertops. When I am not using all of the burners on the stove, the Camco 43547 Bamboo Stove Top Work Surface - 2 Burner comes in handy. It fits vertically over two burners, giving me a flat surface where I can set things down, rather than directly on a burner.

The board is thick and seems to be of excellent quality. It does require assembly when you receive it – there are four feet that install on each corner so the board is raised up off of the stove burners. The instructions provided for this installation are poor, without much direction or a good photograph. Camco’s website provided no help with this either. If it had not been for the color photographs on the Amazon page for this product, I don’t know if I would have figured this out – at least not as quickly as I did. From these photos, I saw how the screw fit and which end of the foot was supposed to fit against the board. They don’t look like they fit properly, but the foot has a recessed area you have to kind of push the screw through in order for it to work.

Once I figured out the trick to installing the first foot, the rest were easy. They are rubbery, so they don’t slide, which would be ideal if you are using this on an RV or a houseboat.

You can also use this as a cutting board, and there is a groove that goes around every side of the board to catch any juices from anything you cut on it. Camco says this surface will not dull knives, and I haven’t noticed otherwise this month.

If you are looking for something to cover the burners in your home or recreational vehicle or boat, I would recommend this product.

All the while I was hoping that the supplied pin or wedge, or whatever you call it, holding the spring in place would not slip thus causing the spring to snap at me. This refrigerator looks interesting, but be careful.
We bought a GE side-by-side refrigerator about five years ago. Since then we have had repeated problems with it, and had to call the GE repairman nine different times. The doors had to be replaced once. The ice maker was replaced four times, one time when it started breaking up and mixing shards of plastic with the ice. We could have been killed.
This last time the ice maker flooded the kitchen within two hours. We called GE for repairs and the repairman came several days later. He installed a new ice maker and said everthing was fine. He turned on the water and said "All set to go." When we checked a few hours later, there was no ice, the kitchen was flooding, and sheets of water were coming down from the ceiling in our rec room. The GE repairman never turned the valve of the water line on completely, so it leaked far far worse than it ever had before. Knowing to turn water valves completely should be Repair 101. I called GE back at 3:15; the operator said she would e-mail an immediate request to the repair people in our area. A taped message contacted us some five days later. By that time we had bought a new, very different brand.
I can understand a product having problems. But when the factory authorized repairmen make the problems far, far worse, that manufacturer should be put out of business.

I have a Kenmore Elite Model 66942500 electric dryer which BTW is a piece of junk. Something breaking on it every couple months!! Luckily, this model is very easy to work on yourself. Gotta love YouTube! Lol. Anyhow, finished up a load of laundry a couple days ago, loaded up more clothes, hit the start button....nothing. Pulled outthe trusty laptop, and went to good ol' YouTube. The general consensus was a blown thermal fuse on blower housing. I located the fuse, disconnected the 2 blue wires and tested for continuity with a meter. Nothing. I then proceeded to bypass the fuse, I touched the wires together and had someone else press start button...started right up so I knew for sure it was fuse. The local appliance store wanted $19.99 for ONE replacement fuse!!! Forget that highway robbery! I found these 6 exact replacements that even paying for expedited shipping was about the same price for OBE LOCALLY. Always always always look for appliance parts online!! Wayyyyyy cheaper! I'd love to see these local shops put right out of business for how much they screw their customers!

I gave it 4 stars because this vent is the only one of its kind and solved my ventilation problem. This product is the only kind of exhaust vent to use if you have perforated or vented soffets. I had been using a roof exhaust vent for my bathroom. It caused all the snow to melt and created a huge ice dam. I changed over to a soffet vent to prevent that problem. If I had used a standard soffet vent then my attic would be destroyed by the condensing water and mold.

There are negatives. The flapper will get frozen shut. I was warned of this by previous reviewers. To minimize the problem I drilled two small holes in the bottom near the flapper. I formed small pieces of 14 ga wire into the holes and under the flapper to keep it from closing all the way. Even with this setup it still froze shut for a few minutes. But the gap allows a little warm air to escape and melt the ice. The vent is also flimsy but was still easy to install. The fit with the duct was tight.

If I had to buy this again I would look for other models. In the absence of any alternatives I would definitely buy again.

When you set it up follow instructions exactly. Clean as recommended or more. We clean it with vinegar. We use ONLY distilled water.

Keep things away 6 inches or more from side vents for airflow.
